title: Material placer and high volume supply hopper
abstract: A material placer having an augmented hopper, which can receive and convey concrete or other material at a greater rate than other material placers known in the industry. The augmented hopper has a relatively large volume, a bumper structure, a hinged flop gate, and a sliding gate plate that improves the process of receiving concrete or other discharged material from delivery trucks. The hinged flop gate and/or the sliding plate can optionally be vibrated to aid in moving concrete or other material into the trough of the augmented hopper. The material placer is further configured to have ease of access for cleaning and maintenance.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A material placer unit, configured to provide concrete or other materials to another machine or a location on a ground surface for further paving, the material placer unit comprising: a central module, comprising: a frame; a transfer conveyor; and one or more jacking columns configured to adjust the height of the frame; a hopper mechanically coupled to the central module, comprising: an auger positioned within a trough; an actuation system; a passageway, with the transfer conveyor being arranged to receive material from the hopper through the passageway; and a gate having a sliding plate, the actuation system being configured to extend and retract the sliding plate along the length of the gate; a pivot yoke mechanically coupled to the central module; and a distribution conveyor mechanically coupled to the pivot yoke, the distribution conveyor having a conveyor belt, and the conveyor belt being arranged receive material from the transfer conveyor. 2. The material placer unit of claim 1, wherein the central module has four jacking columns located at corners of the frame, configured to adjust the height of the frame. 3. The material placer unit of claim 1, an access door that provides for access to interior machinery of the material placer unit. 4. The material placer unit of claim 1 , wherein the distribution conveyor has a range of motion relative to the central module of 170Â°. 5. The material placer unit of claim 1, wherein the actuation system is further configured to vibrate the sliding plate. 6. The material placer unit of claim 1 , wherein the transfer conveyor and the distribution conveyor are both configured to receive a material that is concrete or a concrete slurry. 7. The material placer unit of claim 1 , wherein the central module further comprises wheels and a motor mounted to the central module, and wherein the material placer unit further comprises a steering control system. 8. The material placer unit of claim 1 , wherein the hopper, the transfer conveyor, and the distribution conveyor provide an onboard volume capacity of about 8.75 cubic yards. 9. The material placer unit of claim 1 , wherein the gate of the hopper is configured to elevate and press up against a bed or chassis of a truck. 10. The material placer unit of claim 9, wherein the gate further comprises a sealing lip configured to press up against the bed or chassis of the truck and prevent the spillage of material between the bed or chassis of the truck and the gate. 11. The material placer unit of claim 1 , wherein a discharge end of the distribution conveyor is configurable to extend above and over barriers that are proximate or adjacent to the material placer unit. 12. An augmented hopper, configured to receive concrete or other materials, comprising: an auger positioned within a trough, the trough formed in part by a back wall of the augmented hopper; an actuation system; a passageway located within the back wall; and a gate having a sliding plate, where the sliding plate is mechanically coupled to the actuation system, and where the actuation system is configured to extend and to retract the sliding plate. 13. The augmented hopper of claim 12, wherein the actuation system is further configured to vibrate the sliding plate. 14. The augmented hopper of claim 12, further comprising vibrators mounted to the gate, vibrators mounted to the back gate, or a combination thereof. 15. The augmented hopper of claim 12, wherein the hopper further comprises a buffer structure having a pressure-activated shock suppression system. 16. The augmented hopper of claim 12, wherein the sliding plate has an upper surface that includes a plurality of triangular projections. 17. The augmented hopper of claim 12, wherein the auger has flighting configured to move material within the trough toward the center of the auger. 18. The augmented hopper of claim 12, wherein the auger has paddles arranged to push the material within the trough toward the passageway. 19. The augmented hopper of claim 12, further comprising a mechanical coupling system configured to mount the augmented hopper to a mobile material distributor. 20. The augmented hopper of claim 12, wherein the sliding plate is beveled on a receiving edge of the sliding plate, a hopper edge of the sliding plate, or both.
